translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the earth was without form, and void; and darkness was upon the face of the deep. And the Spirit of God moved upon the face of the waters.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God said, Let there be light: and there was light.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God saw the light, that it was good: and God divided the light from the darkness.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God called the light Day, and the darkness he called Night. And the evening and the morning were the first day.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God said, Let there be a firmament in the midst of the waters, and let it divide the waters from the waters.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God made the firmament, and divided the waters which were under the firmament from the waters which were above the firmament: and it was so.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God called the firmament Heaven. And the evening and the morning were the second day.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God said, Let the waters under the heaven be gathered together unto one place, and let the dry land appear: and it was so.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God called the dry land Earth; and the gathering together of the waters called he Seas: and God saw that it was good.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God said, Let the earth bring forth grass, the herb yielding seed, and the fruit tree yielding fruit after his kind, whose seed is in itself, upon the earth: and it was so.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the earth brought forth grass, and herb yielding seed after his kind, and the tree yielding fruit, whose seed was in itself, after his kind: and God saw that it was good.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the evening and the morning were the third day.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God said, Let there be lights in the firmament of the heaven to divide the day from the night; and let them be for signs, and for seasons, and for days, and years:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And let them be for lights in the firmament of the heaven to give light upon the earth: and it was so.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God made two great lights; the greater light to rule the day, and the lesser light to rule the night: he made the stars also.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God set them in the firmament of the heaven to give light upon the earth,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And to rule over the day and over the night, and to divide the light from the darkness: and God saw that it was good.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the evening and the morning were the fourth day.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God said, Let the waters bring forth abundantly the moving creature that hath life, and fowl that may fly above the earth in the open firmament of heaven.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God created great whales, and every living creature that moveth, which the waters brought forth abundantly, after their kind, and every winged fowl after his kind: and God saw that it was good.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God blessed them, saying, Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let fowl multiply in the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the evening and the morning were the fifth day.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God said, Let the earth bring forth the living creature after his kind, cattle, and creeping thing, and beast of the earth after his kind: and it was so.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God made the beast of the earth after his kind, and cattle after their kind, and every thing that creepeth upon the earth after his kind: and God saw that it was good.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God said, Let us make man in our image, after our likeness: and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over the cattle, and over all the earth, and over every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So God created man in his own image, in the image of God created he him; male and female created he them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God blessed them, and God said unto them, Be fruitful, and multiply, and replenish the earth, and subdue it: and have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over every living thing that moveth upon the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God said, Behold, I have given you every herb bearing seed, which is upon the face of all the earth, and every tree, in the which is the fruit of a tree yielding seed; to you it shall be for meat.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And to every beast of the earth, and to every fowl of the air, and to every thing that creepeth upon the earth, wherein there is life, I have given every green herb for meat: and it was so.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God saw every thing that he had made, and, behold, it was very good. And the evening and the morning were the sixth day.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thus the heavens and the earth were finished, and all the host of them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And on the seventh day God ended his work which he had made; and he rested on the seventh day from all his work which he had made.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God blessed the seventh day, and sanctified it: because that in it he had rested from all his work which God created and made.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: These are the generations of the heavens and of the earth when they were created, in the day that the LORD God made the earth and the heavens,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And every plant of the field before it was in the earth, and every herb of the field before it grew: for the LORD God had not caused it to rain upon the earth, and there was not a man to till the ground.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But there went up a mist from the earth, and watered the whole face of the ground.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD God formed man of the dust of the ground, and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and man became a living soul.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD God planted a garden eastward in Eden; and there he put the man whom he had formed.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And out of the ground made the LORD God to grow every tree that is pleasant to the sight, and good for food; the tree of life also in the midst of the garden, and the tree of knowledge of good and evil.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And a river went out of Eden to water the garden; and from thence it was parted, and became into four heads.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The name of the first is Pison: that is it which compasseth the whole land of Havilah, where there is gold;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the gold of that land is good: there is bdellium and the onyx stone.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the name of the second river is Gihon: the same is it that compasseth the whole land of Ethiopia.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the name of the third river is Hiddekel: that is it which goeth toward the east of Assyria. And the fourth river is Euphrates.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD God took the man, and put him into the garden of Eden to dress it and to keep it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD God commanded the man, saying, Of every tree of the garden thou mayest freely eat:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, thou shalt not eat of it: for in the day that thou eatest thereof thou shalt surely die.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD God said, It is not good that the man should be alone; I will make him an help meet for him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And out of the ground the LORD God formed every beast of the field, and every fowl of the air; and brought them unto Adam to see what he would call them: and whatsoever Adam called every living creature, that was the name thereof.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Adam gave names to all cattle, and to the fowl of the air, and to every beast of the field; but for Adam there was not found an help meet for him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD God caused a deep sleep to fall upon Adam, and he slept: and he took one of his ribs, and closed up the flesh instead thereof;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the rib, which the LORD God had taken from man, made he a woman, and brought her unto the man.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Adam said, This is now bone of my bones, and flesh of my flesh: she shall be called Woman, because she was taken out of Man.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore shall a man leave his father and his mother, and shall cleave unto his wife: and they shall be one flesh.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And they were both naked, the man and his wife, and were not ashamed.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now the serpent was more subtil than any beast of the field which the LORD God had made. And he said unto the woman, Yea, hath God said, Ye shall not eat of every tree of the garden?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the woman said unto the serpent, We may eat of the fruit of the trees of the garden:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But of the fruit of the tree which is in the midst of the garden, God hath said, Ye shall not eat of it, neither shall ye touch it, lest ye die.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the serpent said unto the woman, Ye shall not surely die:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For God doth know that in the day ye eat thereof, then your eyes shall be opened, and ye shall be as gods, knowing good and evil.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And when the woman saw that the tree was good for food, and that it was pleasant to the eyes, and a tree to be desired to make one wise, she took of the fruit thereof, and did eat, and gave also unto her husband with her; and he did eat.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the eyes of them both were opened, and they knew that they were naked; and they sewed fig leaves together, and made themselves aprons.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And they heard the voice of the LORD God walking in the garden in the cool of the day: and Adam and his wife hid themselves from the presence of the LORD God amongst the trees of the garden.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD God called unto Adam, and said unto him, Where art thou?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he said, I heard thy voice in the garden, and I was afraid, because I was naked; and I hid myself.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he said, Who told thee that thou wast naked? Hast thou eaten of the tree, whereof I commanded thee that thou shouldest not eat?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the man said, The woman whom thou gavest to be with me, she gave me of the tree, and I did eat.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD God said unto the woman, What is this that thou hast done? And the woman said, The serpent beguiled me, and I did eat.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD God said unto the serpent, Because thou hast done this, thou art cursed above all cattle, and above every beast of the field; upon thy belly shalt thou go, and dust shalt thou eat all the days of thy life:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And I will put enmity between thee and the woman, and between thy seed and her seed; it shall bruise thy head, and thou shalt bruise his heel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Unto the woman he said, I will greatly multiply thy sorrow and thy conception; in sorrow thou shalt bring forth children; and thy desire shall be to thy husband, and he shall rule over thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And unto Adam he said, Because thou hast hearkened unto the voice of thy wife, and hast eaten of the tree, of which I commanded thee, saying, Thou shalt not eat of it: cursed is the ground for thy sake; in sorrow shalt thou eat of it all the days of thy life;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thorns also and thistles shall it bring forth to thee; and thou shalt eat the herb of the field;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: In the sweat of thy face shalt thou eat bread, till thou return unto the ground; for out of it wast thou taken: for dust thou art, and unto dust shalt thou return.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Adam called his wife’s name Eve; because she was the mother of all living.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Unto Adam also and to his wife did the LORD God make coats of skins, and clothed them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD God said, Behold, the man is become as one of us, to know good and evil: and now, lest he put forth his hand, and take also of the tree of life, and eat, and live for ever:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore the LORD God sent him forth from the garden of Eden, to till the ground from whence he was taken.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So he drove out the man; and he placed at the east of the garden of Eden Cherubims, and a flaming sword which turned every way, to keep the way of the tree of life.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Adam knew Eve his wife; and she conceived, and bare Cain, and said, I have gotten a man from the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And she again bare his brother Abel. And Abel was a keeper of sheep, but Cain was a tiller of the ground.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And in process of time it came to pass, that Cain brought of the fruit of the ground an offering unto the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Abel, he also brought of the firstlings of his flock and of the fat thereof. And the LORD had respect unto Abel and to his offering:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But unto Cain and to his offering he had not respect. And Cain was very wroth, and his countenance fell.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD said unto Cain, Why art thou wroth? and why is thy countenance fallen?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If thou doest well, shalt thou not be accepted? and if thou doest not well, sin lieth at the door. And unto thee shall be his desire, and thou shalt rule over him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Cain talked with Abel his brother: and it came to pass, when they were in the field, that Cain rose up against Abel his brother, and slew him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD said unto Cain, Where is Abel thy brother? And he said, I know not: Am I my brother’s keeper?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he said, What hast thou done? the voice of thy brother’s blood crieth unto me from the ground.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And now art thou cursed from the earth, which hath opened her mouth to receive thy brother’s blood from thy hand;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When thou tillest the ground, it shall not henceforth yield unto thee her strength; a fugitive and a vagabond shalt thou be in the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Cain said unto the LORD, My punishment is greater than I can bear.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Behold, thou hast driven me out this day from the face of the earth; and from thy face shall I be hid; and I shall be a fugitive and a vagabond in the earth; and it shall come to pass, that every one that findeth me shall slay me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD said unto him, Therefore whosoever slayeth Cain, vengeance shall be taken on him sevenfold. And the LORD set a mark upon Cain, lest any finding him should kill him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Cain went out from the presence of the LORD, and dwelt in the land of Nod, on the east of Eden.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Cain knew his wife; and she conceived, and bare Enoch: and he builded a city, and called the name of the city, after the name of his son, Enoch.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And unto Enoch was born Irad: and Irad begat Mehujael: and Mehujael begat Methusael: and Methusael begat Lamech.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Lamech took unto him two wives: the name of the one was Adah, and the name of the other Zillah.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Adah bare Jabal: he was the father of such as dwell in tents, and of such as have cattle.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And his brother’s name was Jubal: he was the father of all such as handle the harp and organ.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Zillah, she also bare Tubalcain, an instructer of every artificer in brass and iron: and the sister of Tubalcain was Naamah.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Lamech said unto his wives, Adah and Zillah, Hear my voice; ye wives of Lamech, hearken unto my speech: for I have slain a man to my wounding, and a young man to my hurt.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: If Cain shall be avenged sevenfold, truly Lamech seventy and sevenfold.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Adam knew his wife again; and she bare a son, and called his name Seth: For God, said she, hath appointed me another seed instead of Abel, whom Cain slew.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And to Seth, to him also there was born a son; and he called his name Enos: then began men to call upon the name of the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: This is the book of the generations of Adam. In the day that God created man, in the likeness of God made he him;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Male and female created he them; and blessed them, and called their name Adam, in the day when they were created.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Adam lived an hundred and thirty years, and begat a son in his own likeness, after his image; and called his name Seth:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the days of Adam after he had begotten Seth were eight hundred years: and he begat sons and daughters:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And all the days that Adam lived were nine hundred and thirty years: and he died.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Seth lived an hundred and five years, and begat Enos:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Seth lived after he begat Enos eight hundred and seven years, and begat sons and daughters:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And all the days of Seth were nine hundred and twelve years: and he died.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Enos lived ninety years, and begat Cainan:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Enos lived after he begat Cainan eight hundred and fifteen years, and begat sons and daughters:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And all the days of Enos were nine hundred and five years: and he died.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Cainan lived seventy years, and begat Mahalaleel:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Cainan lived after he begat Mahalaleel eight hundred and forty years, and begat sons and daughters:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And all the days of Cainan were nine hundred and ten years: and he died.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Mahalaleel lived sixty and five years, and begat Jared:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Mahalaleel lived after he begat Jared eight hundred and thirty years, and begat sons and daughters:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And all the days of Mahalaleel were eight hundred ninety and five years: and he died.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Jared lived an hundred sixty and two years, and he begat Enoch:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Jared lived after he begat Enoch eight hundred years, and begat sons and daughters:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And all the days of Jared were nine hundred sixty and two years: and he died.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Enoch lived sixty and five years, and begat Methuselah:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Enoch walked with God after he begat Methuselah three hundred years, and begat sons and daughters:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And all the days of Enoch were three hundred sixty and five years:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Enoch walked with God: and he was not; for God took him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Methuselah lived an hundred eighty and seven years, and begat Lamech:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Methuselah lived after he begat Lamech seven hundred eighty and two years, and begat sons and daughters:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And all the days of Methuselah were nine hundred sixty and nine years: and he died.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Lamech lived an hundred eighty and two years, and begat a son:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he called his name Noah, saying, This same shall comfort us concerning our work and toil of our hands, because of the ground which the LORD hath cursed.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Lamech lived after he begat Noah five hundred ninety and five years, and begat sons and daughters:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And all the days of Lamech were seven hundred seventy and seven years: and he died.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Noah was five hundred years old: and Noah begat Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it came to pass, when men began to multiply on the face of the earth, and daughters were born unto them,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: That the sons of God saw the daughters of men that they were fair; and they took them wives of all which they chose.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD said, My spirit shall not always strive with man, for that he also is flesh: yet his days shall be an hundred and twenty years.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: There were giants in the earth in those days; and also after that, when the sons of God came in unto the daughters of men, and they bare children to them, the same became mighty men which were of old, men of renown.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God saw that the wickedness of man was great in the earth, and that every imagination of the thoughts of his heart was only evil continually.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it repented the LORD that he had made man on the earth, and it grieved him at his heart.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD said, I will destroy man whom I have created from the face of the earth; both man, and beast, and the creeping thing, and the fowls of the air; for it repenteth me that I have made them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But Noah found grace in the eyes of the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: These are the generations of Noah: Noah was a just man and perfect in his generations, and Noah walked with God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Noah begat three sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The earth also was corrupt before God, and the earth was filled with violence.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God looked upon the earth, and, behold, it was corrupt; for all flesh had corrupted his way upon the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God said unto Noah, The end of all flesh is come before me; for the earth is filled with violence through them; and, behold, I will destroy them with the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Make thee an ark of gopher wood; rooms shalt thou make in the ark, and shalt pitch it within and without with pitch.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And this is the fashion which thou shalt make it of: The length of the ark shall be three hundred cubits, the breadth of it fifty cubits, and the height of it thirty cubits.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: A window shalt thou make to the ark, and in a cubit shalt thou finish it above; and the door of the ark shalt thou set in the side thereof; with lower, second, and third stories shalt thou make it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And, behold, I, even I, do bring a flood of waters upon the earth, to destroy all flesh, wherein is the breath of life, from under heaven; and every thing that is in the earth shall die.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But with thee will I establish my covenant; and thou shalt come into the ark, thou, and thy sons, and thy wife, and thy sons’ wives with thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And of every living thing of all flesh, two of every sort shalt thou bring into the ark, to keep them alive with thee; they shall be male and female.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of fowls after their kind, and of cattle after their kind, of every creeping thing of the earth after his kind, two of every sort shall come unto thee, to keep them alive.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And take thou unto thee of all food that is eaten, and thou shalt gather it to thee; and it shall be for food for thee, and for them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thus did Noah; according to all that God commanded him, so did he.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD said unto Noah, Come thou and all thy house into the ark; for thee have I seen righteous before me in this generation.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of every clean beast thou shalt take to thee by sevens, the male and his female: and of beasts that are not clean by two, the male and his female.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of fowls also of the air by sevens, the male and the female; to keep seed alive upon the face of all the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For yet seven days, and I will cause it to rain upon the earth forty days and forty nights; and every living substance that I have made will I destroy from off the face of the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Noah did according unto all that the LORD commanded him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Noah was six hundred years old when the flood of waters was upon the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Noah went in, and his sons, and his wife, and his sons’ wives with him, into the ark, because of the waters of the flood.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Of clean beasts, and of beasts that are not clean, and of fowls, and of every thing that creepeth upon the earth,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: There went in two and two unto Noah into the ark, the male and the female, as God had commanded Noah.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it came to pass after seven days, that the waters of the flood were upon the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: In the six hundredth year of Noah’s life, in the second month, the seventeenth day of the month, the same day were all the fountains of the great deep broken up, and the windows of heaven were opened.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the rain was upon the earth forty days and forty nights.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: In the selfsame day entered Noah, and Shem, and Ham, and Japheth, the sons of Noah, and Noah’s wife, and the three wives of his sons with them, into the ark;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They, and every beast after his kind, and all the cattle after their kind, and every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth after his kind, and every fowl after his kind, every bird of every sort.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And they went in unto Noah into the ark, two and two of all flesh, wherein is the breath of life.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And they that went in, went in male and female of all flesh, as God had commanded him: and the LORD shut him in.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the flood was forty days upon the earth; and the waters increased, and bare up the ark, and it was lift up above the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the waters prevailed, and were increased greatly upon the earth; and the ark went upon the face of the waters.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the waters prevailed exceedingly upon the earth; and all the high hills, that were under the whole heaven, were covered.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Fifteen cubits upward did the waters prevail; and the mountains were covered.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And all flesh died that moved upon the earth, both of fowl, and of cattle, and of beast, and of every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth, and every man:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: All in whose nostrils was the breath of life, of all that was in the dry land, died.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And every living substance was destroyed which was upon the face of the ground, both man, and cattle, and the creeping things, and the fowl of the heaven; and they were destroyed from the earth: and Noah only remained alive, and they that were with him in the ark.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the waters prevailed upon the earth an hundred and fifty days.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God remembered Noah, and every living thing, and all the cattle that was with him in the ark: and God made a wind to pass over the earth, and the waters asswaged;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The fountains also of the deep and the windows of heaven were stopped, and the rain from heaven was restrained;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the waters returned from off the earth continually: and after the end of the hundred and fifty days the waters were abated.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the ark rested in the seventh month, on the seventeenth day of the month, upon the mountains of Ararat.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the waters decreased continually until the tenth month: in the tenth month, on the first day of the month, were the tops of the mountains seen.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it came to pass at the end of forty days, that Noah opened the window of the ark which he had made:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he sent forth a raven, which went forth to and fro, until the waters were dried up from off the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Also he sent forth a dove from him, to see if the waters were abated from off the face of the ground;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But the dove found no rest for the sole of her foot, and she returned unto him into the ark, for the waters were on the face of the whole earth: then he put forth his hand, and took her, and pulled her in unto him into the ark.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he stayed yet other seven days; and again he sent forth the dove out of the ark;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the dove came in to him in the evening; and, lo, in her mouth was an olive leaf pluckt off: so Noah knew that the waters were abated from off the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he stayed yet other seven days; and sent forth the dove; which returned not again unto him any more.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it came to pass in the six hundredth and first year, in the first month, the first day of the month, the waters were dried up from off the earth: and Noah removed the covering of the ark, and looked, and, behold, the face of the ground was dry.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And in the second month, on the seven and twentieth day of the month, was the earth dried.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God spake unto Noah, saying,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Go forth of the ark, thou, and thy wife, and thy sons, and thy sons’ wives with thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Bring forth with thee every living thing that is with thee, of all flesh, both of fowl, and of cattle, and of every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth; that they may breed abundantly in the earth, and be fruitful, and multiply upon the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Noah went forth, and his sons, and his wife, and his sons’ wives with him:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Every beast, every creeping thing, and every fowl, and whatsoever creepeth upon the earth, after their kinds, went forth out of the ark.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Noah builded an altar unto the LORD; and took of every clean beast, and of every clean fowl, and offered burnt offerings on the altar.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD smelled a sweet savour; and the LORD said in his heart, I will not again curse the ground any more for man’s sake; for the imagination of man’s heart is evil from his youth; neither will I again smite any more every thing living, as I have done.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: While the earth remaineth, seedtime and harvest, and cold and heat, and summer and winter, and day and night shall not cease.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God blessed Noah and his sons, and said unto them, Be fruitful, and multiply, and replenish the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the fear of you and the dread of you shall be upon every beast of the earth, and upon every fowl of the air, upon all that moveth upon the earth, and upon all the fishes of the sea; into your hand are they delivered.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Every moving thing that liveth shall be meat for you; even as the green herb have I given you all things.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But flesh with the life thereof, which is the blood thereof, shall ye not eat.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And surely your blood of your lives will I require; at the hand of every beast will I require it, and at the hand of man; at the hand of every man’s brother will I require the life of man.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whoso sheddeth man’s blood, by man shall his blood be shed: for in the image of God made he man.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And you, be ye fruitful, and multiply; bring forth abundantly in the earth, and multiply therein.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God spake unto Noah, and to his sons with him, saying,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And I, behold, I establish my covenant with you, and with your seed after you;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And with every living creature that is with you, of the fowl, of the cattle, and of every beast of the earth with you; from all that go out of the ark, to every beast of the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And I will establish my covenant with you; neither shall all flesh be cut off any more by the waters of a flood; neither shall there any more be a flood to destroy the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God said, This is the token of the covenant which I make between me and you and every living creature that is with you, for perpetual generations:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I do set my bow in the cloud, and it shall be for a token of a covenant between me and the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, when I bring a cloud over the earth, that the bow shall be seen in the cloud:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And I will remember my covenant, which is between me and you and every living creature of all flesh; and the waters shall no more become a flood to destroy all flesh.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the bow shall be in the cloud; and I will look upon it, that I may remember the everlasting covenant between God and every living creature of all flesh that is upon the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God said unto Noah, This is the token of the covenant, which I have established between me and all flesh that is upon the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the sons of Noah, that went forth of the ark, were Shem, and Ham, and Japheth: and Ham is the father of Canaan.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: These are the three sons of Noah: and of them was the whole earth overspread.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Noah began to be an husbandman, and he planted a vineyard:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he drank of the wine, and was drunken; and he was uncovered within his tent.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Ham, the father of Canaan, saw the nakedness of his father, and told his two brethren without.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Shem and Japheth took a garment, and laid it upon both their shoulders, and went backward, and covered the nakedness of their father; and their faces were backward, and they saw not their father’s nakedness.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Noah awoke from his wine, and knew what his younger son had done unto him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he said, Cursed be Canaan; a servant of servants shall he be unto his brethren.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he said, Blessed be the LORD God of Shem; and Canaan shall be his servant.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: God shall enlarge Japheth, and he shall dwell in the tents of Shem; and Canaan shall be his servant.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Noah lived after the flood three hundred and fifty years.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And all the days of Noah were nine hundred and fifty years: and he died.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now these are the generations of the sons of Noah, Shem, Ham, and Japheth: and unto them were sons born after the flood.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The sons of Japheth; Gomer, and Magog, and Madai, and Javan, and Tubal, and Meshech, and Tiras.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the sons of Gomer; Ashkenaz, and Riphath, and Togarmah.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the sons of Javan; Elishah, and Tarshish, Kittim, and Dodanim.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: By these were the isles of the Gentiles divided in their lands; every one after his tongue, after their families, in their nations.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the sons of Ham; Cush, and Mizraim, and Phut, and Canaan.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the sons of Cush; Seba, and Havilah, and Sabtah, and Raamah, and Sabtechah: and the sons of Raamah; Sheba, and Dedan.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Cush begat Nimrod: he began to be a mighty one in the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He was a mighty hunter before the LORD: wherefore it is said, Even as Nimrod the mighty hunter before the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the beginning of his kingdom was Babel, and Erech, and Accad, and Calneh, in the land of Shinar.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Out of that land went forth Asshur, and builded Nineveh, and the city Rehoboth, and Calah,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Resen between Nineveh and Calah: the same is a great city.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Mizraim begat Ludim, and Anamim, and Lehabim, and Naphtuhim,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Pathrusim, and Casluhim, (out of whom came Philistim,) and Caphtorim.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Canaan begat Sidon his first born, and Heth,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the Jebusite, and the Amorite, and the Girgasite,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the Hivite, and the Arkite, and the Sinite,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the Arvadite, and the Zemarite, and the Hamathite: and afterward were the families of the Canaanites spread abroad.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the border of the Canaanites was from Sidon, as thou comest to Gerar, unto Gaza; as thou goest, unto Sodom, and Gomorrah, and Admah, and Zeboim, even unto Lasha.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: These are the sons of Ham, after their families, after their tongues, in their countries, and in their nations.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Unto Shem also, the father of all the children of Eber, the brother of Japheth the elder, even to him were children born.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The children of Shem; Elam, and Asshur, and Arphaxad, and Lud, and Aram.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the children of Aram; Uz, and Hul, and Gether, and Mash.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Arphaxad begat Salah; and Salah begat Eber.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And unto Eber were born two sons: the name of one was Peleg; for in his days was the earth divided; and his brother’s name was Joktan.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Joktan begat Almodad, and Sheleph, and Hazarmaveth, and Jerah,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Hadoram, and Uzal, and Diklah,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Obal, and Abimael, and Sheba,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Ophir, and Havilah, and Jobab: all these were the sons of Joktan.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And their dwelling was from Mesha, as thou goest unto Sephar a mount of the east.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: These are the sons of Shem, after their families, after their tongues, in their lands, after their nations.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: These are the families of the sons of Noah, after their generations, in their nations: and by these were the nations divided in the earth after the flood.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the whole earth was of one language, and of one speech.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it came to pass, as they journeyed from the east, that they found a plain in the land of Shinar; and they dwelt there.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And they said one to another, Go to, let us make brick, and burn them thoroughly. And they had brick for stone, and slime had they for morter.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And they said, Go to, let us build us a city and a tower, whose top may reach unto heaven; and let us make us a name, lest we be scattered abroad upon the face of the whole earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD came down to see the city and the tower, which the children of men builded.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD said, Behold, the people is one, and they have all one language; and this they begin to do: and now nothing will be restrained from them, which they have imagined to do.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Go to, let us go down, and there confound their language, that they may not understand one another’s speech.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So the LORD scattered them abroad from thence upon the face of all the earth: and they left off to build the city.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore is the name of it called Babel; because the LORD did there confound the language of all the earth: and from thence did the LORD scatter them abroad upon the face of all the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: These are the generations of Shem: Shem was an hundred years old, and begat Arphaxad two years after the flood:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Shem lived after he begat Arphaxad five hundred years, and begat sons and daughters.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Arphaxad lived five and thirty years, and begat Salah:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Arphaxad lived after he begat Salah four hundred and three years, and begat sons and daughters.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Salah lived thirty years, and begat Eber:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Salah lived after he begat Eber four hundred and three years, and begat sons and daughters.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Eber lived four and thirty years, and begat Peleg:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Eber lived after he begat Peleg four hundred and thirty years, and begat sons and daughters.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Peleg lived thirty years, and begat Reu:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Peleg lived after he begat Reu two hundred and nine years, and begat sons and daughters.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Reu lived two and thirty years, and begat Serug:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Reu lived after he begat Serug two hundred and seven years, and begat sons and daughters.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Serug lived thirty years, and begat Nahor:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Serug lived after he begat Nahor two hundred years, and begat sons and daughters.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Nahor lived nine and twenty years, and begat Terah:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Nahor lived after he begat Terah an hundred and nineteen years, and begat sons and daughters.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Terah lived seventy years, and begat Abram, Nahor, and Haran.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now these are the generations of Terah: Terah begat Abram, Nahor, and Haran; and Haran begat Lot.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Haran died before his father Terah in the land of his nativity, in Ur of the Chaldees.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Abram and Nahor took them wives: the name of Abram’s wife was Sarai; and the name of Nahor’s wife, Milcah, the daughter of Haran, the father of Milcah, and the father of Iscah.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But Sarai was barren; she had no child.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Terah took Abram his son, and Lot the son of Haran his son’s son, and Sarai his daughter in law, his son Abram’s wife; and they went forth with them from Ur of the Chaldees, to go into the land of Canaan; and they came unto Haran, and dwelt there.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the days of Terah were two hundred and five years: and Terah died in Haran.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now the LORD had said unto Abram, Get thee out of thy country, and from thy kindred, and from thy father’s house, unto a land that I will shew thee:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And I will make of thee a great nation, and I will bless thee, and make thy name great; and thou shalt be a blessing:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And I will bless them that bless thee, and curse him that curseth thee: and in thee shall all families of the earth be blessed.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So Abram departed, as the LORD had spoken unto him; and Lot went with him: and Abram was seventy and five years old when he departed out of Haran.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Abram took Sarai his wife, and Lot his brother’s son, and all their substance that they had gathered, and the souls that they had gotten in Haran; and they went forth to go into the land of Canaan; and into the land of Canaan they came.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Abram passed through the land unto the place of Sichem, unto the plain of Moreh. And the Canaanite was then in the land.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD appeared unto Abram, and said, Unto thy seed will I give this land: and there builded he an altar unto the LORD, who appeared unto him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he removed from thence unto a mountain on the east of Bethel, and pitched his tent, having Bethel on the west, and Hai on the east: and there he builded an altar unto the LORD, and called upon the name of the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Abram journeyed, going on still toward the south.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And there was a famine in the land: and Abram went down into Egypt to sojourn there; for the famine was grievous in the land.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it came to pass, when he was come near to enter into Egypt, that he said unto Sarai his wife, Behold now, I know that thou art a fair woman to look upon:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore it shall come to pass, when the Egyptians shall see thee, that they shall say, This is his wife: and they will kill me, but they will save thee alive.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Say, I pray thee, thou art my sister: that it may be well with me for thy sake; and my soul shall live because of thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it came to pass, that, when Abram was come into Egypt, the Egyptians beheld the woman that she was very fair.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The princes also of Pharaoh saw her, and commended her before Pharaoh: and the woman was taken into Pharaoh’s house.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he entreated Abram well for her sake: and he had sheep, and oxen, and he asses, and menservants, and maidservants, and she asses, and camels.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD plagued Pharaoh and his house with great plagues because of Sarai Abram’s wife.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Pharaoh called Abram, and said, What is this that thou hast done unto me? why didst thou not tell me that she was thy wife?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Why saidst thou, She is my sister? so I might have taken her to me to wife: now therefore behold thy wife, take her, and go thy way.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Pharaoh commanded his men concerning him: and they sent him away, and his wife, and all that he had.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Abram went up out of Egypt, he, and his wife, and all that he had, and Lot with him, into the south.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Abram was very rich in cattle, in silver, and in gold.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he went on his journeys from the south even to Bethel, unto the place where his tent had been at the beginning, between Bethel and Hai;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Unto the place of the altar, which he had made there at the first: and there Abram called on the name of the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Lot also, which went with Abram, had flocks, and herds, and tents.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the land was not able to bear them, that they might dwell together: for their substance was great, so that they could not dwell together.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And there was a strife between the herdmen of Abram’s cattle and the herdmen of Lot’s cattle: and the Canaanite and the Perizzite dwelled then in the land.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Abram said unto Lot, Let there be no strife, I pray thee, between me and thee, and between my herdmen and thy herdmen; for we be brethren.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Is not the whole land before thee? separate thyself, I pray thee, from me: if thou wilt take the left hand, then I will go to the right; or if thou depart to the right hand, then I will go to the left.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Lot lifted up his eyes, and beheld all the plain of Jordan, that it was well watered every where, before the LORD destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah, even as the garden of the LORD, like the land of Egypt, as thou comest unto Zoar.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then Lot chose him all the plain of Jordan; and Lot journeyed east: and they separated themselves the one from the other.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Abram dwelled in the land of Canaan, and Lot dwelled in the cities of the plain, and pitched his tent toward Sodom.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But the men of Sodom were wicked and sinners before the LORD exceedingly.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD said unto Abram, after that Lot was separated from him, Lift up now thine eyes, and look from the place where thou art northward, and southward, and eastward, and westward:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For all the land which thou seest, to thee will I give it, and to thy seed for ever.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And I will make thy seed as the dust of the earth: so that if a man can number the dust of the earth, then shall thy seed also be numbered.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Arise, walk through the land in the length of it and in the breadth of it; for I will give it unto thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then Abram removed his tent, and came and dwelt in the plain of Mamre, which is in Hebron, and built there an altar unto the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it came to pass in the days of Amraphel king of Shinar, Arioch king of Ellasar, Chedorlaomer king of Elam, and Tidal king of nations;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: That these made war with Bera king of Sodom, and with Birsha king of Gomorrah, Shinab king of Admah, and Shemeber king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela, which is Zoar.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: All these were joined together in the vale of Siddim, which is the salt sea.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Twelve years they served Chedorlaomer, and in the thirteenth year they rebelled.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And in the fourteenth year came Chedorlaomer, and the kings that were with him, and smote the Rephaims in Ashteroth Karnaim, and the Zuzims in Ham, and the Emims in Shaveh Kiriathaim,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the Horites in their mount Seir, unto Elparan, which is by the wilderness.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And they returned, and came to Enmishpat, which is Kadesh, and smote all the country of the Amalekites, and also the Amorites, that dwelt in Hazezontamar.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And there went out the king of Sodom, and the king of Gomorrah, and the king of Admah, and the king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela (the same is Zoar;) and they joined battle with them in the vale of Siddim;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: With Chedorlaomer the king of Elam, and with Tidal king of nations, and Amraphel king of Shinar, and Arioch king of Ellasar; four kings with five.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the vale of Siddim was full of slimepits; and the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah fled, and fell there; and they that remained fled to the mountain.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And they took all the goods of Sodom and Gomorrah, and all their victuals, and went their way.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And they took Lot, Abram’s brother’s son, who dwelt in Sodom, and his goods, and departed.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And there came one that had escaped, and told Abram the Hebrew; for he dwelt in the plain of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol, and brother of Aner: and these were confederate with Abram.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And when Abram heard that his brother was taken captive, he armed his trained servants, born in his own house, three hundred and eighteen, and pursued them unto Dan.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he divided himself against them, he and his servants, by night, and smote them, and pursued them unto Hobah, which is on the left hand of Damascus.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he brought back all the goods, and also brought again his brother Lot, and his goods, and the women also, and the people.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the king of Sodom went out to meet him after his return from the slaughter of Chedorlaomer, and of the kings that were with him, at the valley of Shaveh, which is the king’s dale.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Melchizedek king of Salem brought forth bread and wine: and he was the priest of the most high God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he blessed him, and said, Blessed be Abram of the most high God, possessor of heaven and earth:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And blessed be the most high God, which hath delivered thine enemies into thy hand. And he gave him tithes of all.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the king of Sodom said unto Abram, Give me the persons, and take the goods to thyself.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Abram said to the king of Sodom, I have lift up mine hand unto the LORD, the most high God, the possessor of heaven and earth,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: That I will not take from a thread even to a shoelatchet, and that I will not take any thing that is thine, lest thou shouldest say, I have made Abram rich:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Save only that which the young men have eaten, and the portion of the men which went with me, Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre; let them take their portion.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: After these things the word of the LORD came unto Abram in a vision, saying, Fear not, Abram: I am thy shield, and thy exceeding great reward.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Abram said, LORD God, what wilt thou give me, seeing I go childless, and the steward of my house is this Eliezer of Damascus?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Abram said, Behold, to me thou hast given no seed: and, lo, one born in my house is mine heir.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And, behold, the word of the LORD came unto him, saying, This shall not be thine heir; but he that shall come forth out of thine own bowels shall be thine heir.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he brought him forth abroad, and said, Look now toward heaven, and tell the stars, if thou be able to number them: and he said unto him, So shall thy seed be.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he believed in the LORD; and he counted it to him for righteousness.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he said unto him, I am the LORD that brought thee out of Ur of the Chaldees, to give thee this land to inherit it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he said, LORD God, whereby shall I know that I shall inherit it?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he said unto him, Take me an heifer of three years old, and a she goat of three years old, and a ram of three years old, and a turtledove, and a young pigeon.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he took unto him all these, and divided them in the midst, and laid each piece one against another: but the birds divided he not.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And when the fowls came down upon the carcases, Abram drove them away.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And when the sun was going down, a deep sleep fell upon Abram; and, lo, an horror of great darkness fell upon him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he said unto Abram, Know of a surety that thy seed shall be a stranger in a land that is not theirs, and shall serve them; and they shall afflict them four hundred years;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And also that nation, whom they shall serve, will I judge: and afterward shall they come out with great substance.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And thou shalt go to thy fathers in peace; thou shalt be buried in a good old age.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But in the fourth generation they shall come hither again: for the iniquity of the Amorites is not yet full.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it came to pass, that, when the sun went down, and it was dark, behold a smoking furnace, and a burning lamp that passed between those pieces.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: In the same day the LORD made a covenant with Abram, saying, Unto thy seed have I given this land, from the river of Egypt unto the great river, the river Euphrates:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The Kenites, and the Kenizzites, and the Kadmonites,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the Hittites, and the Perizzites, and the Rephaims,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the Amorites, and the Canaanites, and the Girgashites, and the Jebusites.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now Sarai Abram’s wife bare him no children: and she had an handmaid, an Egyptian, whose name was Hagar.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Sarai said unto Abram, Behold now, the LORD hath restrained me from bearing: I pray thee, go in unto my maid; it may be that I may obtain children by her. And Abram hearkened to the voice of Sarai.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Sarai Abram’s wife took Hagar her maid the Egyptian, after Abram had dwelt ten years in the land of Canaan, and gave her to her husband Abram to be his wife.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he went in unto Hagar, and she conceived: and when she saw that she had conceived, her mistress was despised in her eyes.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Sarai said unto Abram, My wrong be upon thee: I have given my maid into thy bosom; and when she saw that she had conceived, I was despised in her eyes: the LORD judge between me and thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But Abram said unto Sarai, Behold, thy maid is in thy hand; do to her as it pleaseth thee. And when Sarai dealt hardly with her, she fled from her face.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the angel of the LORD found her by a fountain of water in the wilderness, by the fountain in the way to Shur.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he said, Hagar, Sarai’s maid, whence camest thou? and whither wilt thou go? And she said, I flee from the face of my mistress Sarai.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the angel of the LORD said unto her, Return to thy mistress, and submit thyself under her hands.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the angel of the LORD said unto her, I will multiply thy seed exceedingly, that it shall not be numbered for multitude.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the angel of the LORD said unto her, Behold, thou art with child, and shalt bear a son, and shalt call his name Ishmael; because the LORD hath heard thy affliction.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he will be a wild man; his hand will be against every man, and every man’s hand against him; and he shall dwell in the presence of all his brethren.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And she called the name of the LORD that spake unto her, Thou God seest me: for she said, Have I also here looked after him that seeth me?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Wherefore the well was called Beerlahairoi; behold, it is between Kadesh and Bered.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Hagar bare Abram a son: and Abram called his son’s name, which Hagar bare, Ishmael.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Abram was fourscore and six years old, when Hagar bare Ishmael to Abram.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And when Abram was ninety years old and nine, the LORD appeared to Abram, and said unto him, I am the Almighty God; walk before me, and be thou perfect.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And I will make my covenant between me and thee, and will multiply thee exceedingly.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Abram fell on his face: and God talked with him, saying,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As for me, behold, my covenant is with thee, and thou shalt be a father of many nations.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Neither shall thy name any more be called Abram, but thy name shall be Abraham; for a father of many nations have I made thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And I will make thee exceeding fruitful, and I will make nations of thee, and kings shall come out of thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And I will establish my covenant between me and thee and thy seed after thee in their generations for an everlasting covenant, to be a God unto thee, and to thy seed after thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And I will give unto thee, and to thy seed after thee, the land wherein thou art a stranger, all the land of Canaan, for an everlasting possession; and I will be their God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God said unto Abraham, Thou shalt keep my covenant therefore, thou, and thy seed after thee in their generations.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: This is my covenant, which ye shall keep, between me and you and thy seed after thee; Every man child among you shall be circumcised.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And ye shall circumcise the flesh of your foreskin; and it shall be a token of the covenant betwixt me and you.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he that is eight days old shall be circumcised among you, every man child in your generations, he that is born in the house, or bought with money of any stranger, which is not of thy seed.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He that is born in thy house, and he that is bought with thy money, must needs be circumcised: and my covenant shall be in your flesh for an everlasting covenant.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the uncircumcised man child whose flesh of his foreskin is not circumcised, that soul shall be cut off from his people; he hath broken my covenant.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And I will bless her, and give thee a son also of her: yea, I will bless her, and she shall be a mother of nations; kings of people shall be of her.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then Abraham fell upon his face, and laughed, and said in his heart, Shall a child be born unto him that is an hundred years old? and shall Sarah, that is ninety years old, bear?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Abraham said unto God, O that Ishmael might live before thee!

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And God said, Sarah thy wife shall bear thee a son indeed; and thou shalt call his name Isaac: and I will establish my covenant with him for an everlasting covenant, and with his seed after him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And as for Ishmael, I have heard thee: Behold, I have blessed him, and will make him fruitful, and will multiply him exceedingly; twelve princes shall he beget, and I will make him a great nation.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But my covenant will I establish with Isaac, which Sarah shall bear unto thee at this set time in the next year.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he left off talking with him, and God went up from Abraham.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Abraham took Ishmael his son, and all that were born in his house, and all that were bought with his money, every male among the men of Abraham’s house; and circumcised the flesh of their foreskin in the selfsame day, as God had said unto him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Abraham was ninety years old and nine, when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Ishmael his son was thirteen years old, when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: In the selfsame day was Abraham circumcised, and Ishmael his son.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And all the men of his house, born in the house, and bought with money of the stranger, were circumcised with him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD appeared unto him in the plains of Mamre: and he sat in the tent door in the heat of the day;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he lift up his eyes and looked, and, lo, three men stood by him: and when he saw them, he ran to meet them from the tent door, and bowed himself toward the ground,

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And said, My LORD, if now I have found favour in thy sight, pass not away, I pray thee, from thy servant:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Let a little water, I pray you, be fetched, and wash your feet, and rest yourselves under the tree:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And I will fetch a morsel of bread, and comfort ye your hearts; after that ye shall pass on: for therefore are ye come to your servant. And they said, So do, as thou hast said.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Abraham hastened into the tent unto Sarah, and said, Make ready quickly three measures of fine meal, knead it, and make cakes upon the hearth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Abraham ran unto the herd, and fetcht a calf tender and good, and gave it unto a young man; and he hasted to dress it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he took butter, and milk, and the calf which he had dressed, and set it before them; and he stood by them under the tree, and they did eat.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And they said unto him, Where is Sarah thy wife? And he said, Behold, in the tent.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And he said, I will certainly return unto thee according to the time of life; and, lo, Sarah thy wife shall have a son. And Sarah heard it in the tent door, which was behind him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now Abraham and Sarah were old and well stricken in age; and it ceased to be with Sarah after the manner of women.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore Sarah laughed within herself, saying, After I am waxed old shall I have pleasure, my lord being old also?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD said unto Abraham, Wherefore did Sarah laugh, saying, Shall I of a surety bear a child, which am old?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Is any thing too hard for the LORD? At the time appointed I will return unto thee, according to the time of life, and Sarah shall have a son.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then Sarah denied, saying, I laughed not; for she was afraid. And he said, Nay; but thou didst laugh.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the men rose up from thence, and looked toward Sodom: and Abraham went with them to bring them on the way.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD said, Shall I hide from Abraham that thing which I do;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Seeing that Abraham shall surely become a great and mighty nation, and all the nations of the earth shall be blessed in him?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For I know him, that he will command his children and his household after him, and they shall keep the way of the LORD, to do justice and judgment; that the LORD may bring upon Abraham that which he hath spoken of him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD said, Because the cry of Sodom and Gomorrah is great, and because their sin is very grievous;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I will go down now, and see whether they have done altogether according to the cry of it, which is come unto me; and if not, I will know.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the men turned their faces from thence, and went toward Sodom: but Abraham stood yet before the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
